quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 11 Quiz 11: Trigonometric Functions — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 11: Trigonometric Functions — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: A circle has radius $15$ cm. Find the arc length subtended by a central angle of $72^{\circ}$.
A sector has radius $8$ inches and central angle $\frac{5\pi}{6}$ radians. Find the area of the sector.
The terminal side of angle $\theta$ passes through $(-5, 12)$. Find $\sin\theta$ and $\tan\theta$.
For $y = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}x\right) + 4$, find the amplitude, period, and midline.
Find the phase shift of $y = \cos\left(2x + \frac{\pi}{3}\right)$.
questions.explanation: Factor: $y = \cos(2(x + \pi/6))$. The $+$ inside means shift left by $\pi/6$.

questions.question: Evaluate $\arccos\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)$.
questions.explanation: $\cos(2\pi/3) = -1/2$. Since $2\pi/3 \in [0, \pi]$, it is the principal value.

questions.question: An object in SHM has equation $d = 8\cos(3\pi t)$. Find the frequency in Hz and the total distance traveled in one period.
A population oscillates between $300$ and $700$ with period $12$ months. At $t=0$, it is at its maximum. Write the equation using cosine.
questions.explanation: Midline $= 500$, Amp $= 200$. Period $12 \to b = \pi/6$. Max at $t=0 \to$ cosine: $y = 200\cos(\frac{\pi}{6}t) + 500$.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 11: arc length, sector area, evaluating all six trig functions at key angles, trig from a point, sinusoidal features, phase shift, inverse trig, SHM, and building trig models from data.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) Arc length $s = r\theta$ and sector area $A = \frac{1}{2}r^2\theta$ require radians, (2) Reference angles and quadrant signs for all six trig functions, (3) Phase shift: factor out $b$ first, (4) Start at max $\to$ cosine; start at min $\to$ negative cosine; start at midline going up $\to$ sine.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Converting degrees to radians before arc length/area, (2) $\csc = 1/\sin$, $\sec = 1/\cos$, $\cot = 1/\tan$, (3) Inverse trig returns a principal value in a restricted range, (4) Building models: midline = (max+min)/2, amplitude = (max−min)/2, $b = 2\pi/T$.
